title: Toad-in-the-hole
instructions: Cut out circle from piece of white bread, melt butter in frying pan or griddle, put bread in pan, crack egg and put in center hole of bread, place circle in pan and toast on both sides.  Once lightly set, flip bread over and cook on other side.  Salt and pepper as desired. Serve with toasted middle.
notes: My grandpa, Henry Solomon, did not do a lot of cooking but had some fast meal specialties from his days as a short order cook in a diner. He took this job when the depression hit and he lost his job as an insurance salesman. He quickly learned about restaurant management and climbed the ladder as a restaurant manager. Before he retired, he was president of the company that owned Foster’s Diners (with the nickel cup of coffee), Foster’s Freezes, and the Cliff House in San Francisco.
This was one of my Grandpa’s quick meals. I loved when he made it for me.
